nameMeanings.name: Martínez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Francisco, Pedro, Miguel, Raul, Mario, Jorge, Roberto, Ruben.  Portuguese Ligia, Paulo, Armanda, Catarina, Gonsalo, Marcio, Wenceslao, Anatolio, Godofredo, Lidio, Sil, Zulmira.

Spanish (Martínez): patronymic from the personal name Martin . This surname has also been established in southern Italy since the period of Spanish dominance there. In the US, it is the tenth most frequent surname. Compare De Martinez .

variants.name: Martin
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

variants.name: Martines
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Francisco, Jose, Manuel, Jesus, Carlos, Juan, Luis, Mario, Miguel, Pablo, Ramon, Ricardo.  Portuguese Joao.  Italian Salvatore, Antonio, Maurizio, Ottavio.

Spanish: variant or altered form of Martínez (see Martinez ). This surname is most common in Mexico and Brazil. It has also been established in southern Italy, mainly in Sicily, since the period of Spanish dominance there.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ruy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Rodrigo .  This form was common in the Middle Ages, being borne, for example, by  El Cid ,  Ruy Diaz de Vivar  (  ?1043–99 ).

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Ruy Martínez de Henestrosa was born in 1194, in Henestrosa de las Quintanillas, Valdeolea, Santander, Spain, his father, Martin Ruiz de Henestrosa, was 46 and his mother, Mayor de Sandoval y Rada, was 22. He had at least 1 son with Elvira Carrillo. He died in 1248, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ruy Martínez de Henestrosa est né en 1194, à Henestrosa de las Quintanillas, Valdeolea, Santander, Espagne, son père, Martin Ruiz de Henestrosa, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Mayor de Sandoval y Rada, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Elvira Carrillo. Il est décédé en 1248, à l’âge de 54 ans.

Er hatte mit Elvira Carrillo mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1248 im Alter von 54 Jahren.

ko.Henestrosa de las Quintanillas, Valdeole: 스페인 산탠더 발데오리 헨스트로자 데 라스 퀸탠이라스
ko.lifeSummary: Ruy Martínez de Henestrosa(이)가 1194년 스페인 산탠더 발데오리 헨스트로자 데 라스 퀸탠이라스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Martin Ruiz de Henestrosa(은)는 46세였고, 그의 어머니 Mayor de Sandoval y Rada(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Elvira Carrillo(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1248년 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ruy Martínez de Henestrosa родился в 1194 г. в местности хинестроса де лас куинтаниллас, валдеолеа, сантандир, Испания, его отцу по имени Martin Ruiz de Henestrosa было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Mayor de Sandoval y Rada, 22.
